description: Phase 9.1 of active-plan.md. One-glance operator health — every panel answers a single 'is this working?' question in green/red. Open this dashboard first thing in the morning; if every panel is green, nothing needs your attention. If anything is red, the panel title names the subsystem and the runbook path to investigate.
title: tickvault — Operator Health (one-glance)

options.content:
## tickvault — Operator Health

Every panel below must be **green** during market hours (09:15-15:30 IST). Any red panel names the runbook path in its description. Data comes exclusively from Prometheus `tv_*` metrics — no LogQL dependency, works even when Loki is down. Auto-refreshes every 30s.

panels.title: App
panels.description: tv_* metric endpoint reachable → app binary is running.

panels.title: QuestDB
panels.description: Persistence target. Red = tv_questdb_connected == 0 → runbook: scripts/auto-fix-clear-spill.sh

panels.title: WebSocket Pool
panels.description: Active connections to Dhan main feed. Target: 5/5. Red = <5 during market hours.

panels.title: Valkey Cache
panels.description: Degraded cache = non-fatal but auth + rate limiter cheaper with it healthy.

panels.title: Token
panels.description: Dhan JWT remaining validity. Red = < 1h left.

panels.title: Circuit Breaker
panels.description: OMS circuit. 0=Closed (healthy), 1=Open (auto-halt), 2=Half-Open.

panels.title: Real-time guarantee score
panels.description: Wave 3-D Item 13 — composite SLO score in [0,1]. Updated every 10s. Pure multiplicative product of 6 dimensions (ws_health, qdb_health, tick_freshness, token_freshness, spill_health, phase2_health). Green ≥ 0.95, Orange [0.80, 0.95), Red < 0.80. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-3-d-error-codes.md

panels.title: Tier 2 — Is data flowing without loss?
Ticks / min
panels.description: Rate of ticks processed by the pipeline. Red = 0 during market hours.

panels.title: Ticks lost (cumulative)
panels.description: ZERO-TICK-LOSS GUARANTEE. Must be 0 during market hours. Red = any loss. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/observability-architecture.md

panels.title: Tick buffer active
panels.description: tv_tick_buffer_size > 0 means ring-buffer in use (QuestDB slow or down). Runbook: scripts/auto-fix-clear-spill.sh

panels.title: Ticks spilled to disk
panels.description: Non-zero = ring buffer overflowed and ticks went to disk spill. Auto-drains on QuestDB recovery.

panels.title: Tier 3 — Is the zero-touch automation healthy?
Cross-segment collisions
panels.description: I-P1-11. Known recurring (NIFTY vs ABB, BANKNIFTY vs ADANIENT). Composite index handles it — green stat here means the gauge is alive.

panels.title: Valkey errors / min
panels.description: Cache errors. Green = < 1/min. Red = persistent cache failures.

panels.title: Daily P&L
panels.description: tv_realized_pnl + tv_unrealized_pnl. Red = > abs(daily_loss_threshold) — auto-halt imminent.

panels.title: Instrument registry entries
panels.description: Total instruments subscribed. Red = 0, Yellow = < 10K (startup), Green = full universe.

panels.title: Depth sequence holes / duplicates / rollbacks (PR #288)
panels.description: tv_depth_sequence_*_total counters. Holes = lost packets. Duplicates = benign replay after reconnect. Rollbacks = server-side reconnect reset.

panels.title: Cross-segment security_id collisions (I-P1-11)
panels.description: tv_instrument_registry_collision_pair — each row is a security_id Dhan reused across two segments. Two known collisions (NIFTY id=13, BANKNIFTY id=25) are expected.

panels.title: Phase 2 preopen buffer entries (plan item K)
panels.description: Gauge set at 09:13 IST Phase 2 trigger time. Low value = upstream tick capture problem. Runbook: docs/runbooks/phase2-empty-plan.md

panels.title: Phase 2 runs today (outcome breakdown)
panels.description: Phase 2 triggers once per trading day at 09:13 IST. Green label = complete, red = empty_plan / failed / dispatch_failed. Runbook: docs/runbooks/phase2-empty-plan.md

panels.title: Phase 2 trigger latency (ms)
panels.description: Max observed lateness of the 09:13 IST Phase 2 wakeup in the last 6h. > 60000ms = either RunImmediate crash-recovery path fired or scheduler was blocked.

panels.title: PrevClose writer drops / 5m
panels.description: Wave 1 HOT-PATH-02. Bucketed by reason (full / closed / uninit). Green = 0. Red = sustained drops — see runbook .claude/rules/project/wave-1-error-codes.md (HOT-PATH-02). Always wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 (audit-findings-2026-04-17.md) — raw counters lie after process restart.

panels.title: Spill drops / 5m
panels.description: Wave 1 Item 0.b. Bucketed by reason (open_failed / write_failed / drain_full / drain_closed / drain_uninit). Green = 0. Red = sustained — see runbook .claude/rules/project/wave-1-error-codes.md. Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart.

panels.title: PrevClose persist drops / 5m
panels.description: Wave 1 PREVCLOSE. Bucketed by reason (full / closed / uninit). Green = 0. Red = persistence backpressure — see runbook .claude/rules/project/wave-1-error-codes.md (PREVCLOSE-01). Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart.

panels.title: Phase2EmitGuard dropped / 24h
panels.description: Wave 1 PHASE2-02. Should always be 0 across a 24h sliding window. Any non-zero value means the Phase 2 dispatcher returned without firing the daily outcome notification (regression in main.rs phase2_scheduler). The companion alert tv-phase2-02-emit-guard-dropped stays firing for 24h after a single drop because the increase()[1d] window slides — operator must wait for the sample to age out or restart Prometheus to silence. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-1-error-codes.md (PHASE2-02). Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart.

panels.title: Spill drain write errors / 5m
panels.description: Wave 1 HOT-PATH-01. Async tokio::fs write_all errors inside the spill drain task — distinct from tv_spill_dropped_total (queue overflow). Each increment = one tick payload lost from the spill file. Green = 0. Red = disk full / mount gone / permission issue. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-1-error-codes.md (HOT-PATH-01). Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart.

panels.title: Telegram dispatch rate (5m)
panels.description: Wave 3-B Item 11. Total Telegram messages dispatched in the last 5 minutes, broken down by severity and whether the event was coalesced (true) or sent immediately (false). Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart. A coalesced=true row of 1 represents one summary message folding many events; coalesced=false counts are direct sends. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-3-error-codes.md.

panels.title: Self-test outcome / 24h (Wave 3-C SELFTEST-01/02)
panels.description: Wave 3-C Item 12. Once-per-trading-day market-open self-test fired at 09:16:00 IST. Counts by result label across the last 24h sliding window. Green=0 means no failures detected; any 'degraded' or 'critical' increment means the operator received the SELFTEST-02 Telegram with a list of failed sub-checks (main feed / depth-20 / depth-200 / order-update / pipeline / recent-tick / QuestDB / token-headroom). Companion alert: tv-selftest-02-failed. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-3-c-error-codes.md. Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart.

panels.title: Depth-dynamic diff: cycles + adds + removes (5m)
panels.description: PR-D 2026-05-02. Per-feed (depth-20-dynamic / depth-200-dynamic) counters from the all-5-dynamic depth pipeline (depth_dynamic_pipeline_v2). cycles_total = non-no-op diff cycles in 5m; adds_total = SIDs subscribed; removes_total = SIDs unsubscribed. All wrapped in increase() per audit-findings Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart. A spike in adds + removes during a 60s cycle = volatile rank-set churn; sustained 0 = either no rank changes (steady state) or pipeline failure (check tv_depth_dynamic_set_size).

panels.title: Depth-dynamic set size (current)
panels.description: PR-D 2026-05-02. Live gauge of the current top-K SID-set size per feed. Expected steady-state: depth-20-dynamic = 250, depth-200-dynamic = 5. A sustained value below capacity for 5+ min during market hours fires the tv-depth-dynamic-set-size-low alert (severity warning) — usually upstream MOVERS-02 writer failure. Note: this is a GAUGE not a counter, so it's safe to display raw (Rule 12 only applies to counters).

panels.title: 28-TF Cascade — Bars sealed/sec (rate)
panels.description: Phase 3 9-box closure (PR §6 Phase 3). Per-second rate of 1s bars sealed (tv_candle_cascade_bars_sealed_total) and downstream derived seals across all 28 derived TFs (tv_candle_cascade_fanout_derived_seals_total). Steady-state expectation: bars_sealed ≈ active_instruments / 60 (since each instrument seals ~once/sec); derived_seals ≈ bars_sealed × (28 / 60) on average because finer TFs (3s, 5s) seal more often than coarser TFs (1mo).

panels.title: 28-TF Cascade — Lag + respawn (24h)
panels.description: Phase 3 9-box closure. tv_candle_cascade_lag_total = bars dropped because the 1s seal queue backed up (capacity miss). tv_candle_cascade_respawn_total = supervisor respawns of the cascade task (panic / unexpected exit). Both should be 0 in steady state. ANY non-zero value in the last 24h is a regression worth investigating; the tv-candle-cascade-lag and tv-candle-cascade-respawn alerts page the operator immediately.

panels.title: Subsystem memory estimate (Wave-5 L18)
panels.description: Per-component RAM estimate (tv_subsystem_memory_estimated_bytes{component}). HYBRID len()*size_of sampled every 10s by the subsystem_memory task — NOT raw RSS (process_resident_memory_bytes is the canonical kernel measure). Sum across components reconciles within +/-5% of process RSS via the test_subsystem_memory_sums_within_5pct_of_process_rss ratchet. Components init to NaN (L124); the alert tv-rss-per-subsystem-high uses unless absent_over_time(...) so unregistered components do not page. Replaces the legacy MEMORY_RSS_ALERT_MB single-process gauge retired 2026-05-08 (L122 / BUG-C2).

panels.title: Subsystem memory sampler heartbeat (L125)
panels.description: Seconds since the subsystem_memory sampler task last refreshed gauges. Healthy <= 10s (one sample interval). Alert fires at >30s (3x interval) gated by tv_market_hours_active==1. If this stays high, the per-component values on the panel above are STALE and not trustworthy.

panels.title: Market-hours active gate (L129)
panels.description: 1.0 during 09:00-15:30 IST, 0.0 otherwise. Quiet-hours gate used by tv-rss-per-subsystem-high and tv-subsystem-memory-sampler-stale — those alerts only fire when this gauge == 1.

panels.title: Aggregator seals emitted (5m)
panels.description: Wave 6 Sub-PR #1 item 1.4i. Sealed candles forwarded from the multi-TF aggregator subscriber task (master switch, 1.4d) to the seal-writer mpsc channel (1.4c) over the last 5 minutes. Wrapped in increase() per Rule 12 — raw counters lie after process restart. Expected: ramps up at 09:15 IST market open, plateaus at ~99K bars/minute (11K instruments x 9 TFs) on minute boundaries, ramps down at 15:30 IST. Companion log line per minute: tail data/logs/app.YYYY-MM-DD.log | grep 'aggregator heartbeat'. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-6-error-codes.md::AGGREGATOR-HB-01.

panels.title: Aggregator drops & lag (5m)
panels.description: Wave 6 Sub-PR #1 item 1.4k. Three drop counters from the multi-TF aggregator pipeline, all wrapped in increase() per Rule 12: (1) tv_seal_mpsc_dropped_total — seal mpsc full (writer task fell behind ~20s at peak burst), (2) tv_aggregator_late_ticks_discarded_total — tick arrived after its bucket sealed, (3) tv_aggregator_tick_lag_total — broadcast::Lagged frames the subscriber missed. Companion panel to 'Aggregator seals emitted (5m)' (1.4i) — when the no-seals alert fires (1.4j), this panel tells the operator which class of drop is causing it. Healthy state: all zero or very low (< 100 / 5m). Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-6-error-codes.md::AGGREGATOR-DROP-01.

panels.title: Aggregator instrument-registration rate (5m)
panels.description: Wave 6 Sub-PR #1 item 1.4m. Increment per 5m of (security_id, exchange_segment) pairs lazily registered by the multi-TF aggregator on first-tick (item 1.4d closure: `agg_clone.pre_populate(...)` when `stats.instrument_found == false`). At 09:15:00 IST market open this spikes rapidly as each subscribed instrument fires its first tick. Healthy ramp: spike to a few thousand in the first 5 minutes, then taper to near-zero by 09:30. Wrapped in increase() per audit-findings Rule 12. A flat 0 line at 09:15:01 IST indicates either (a) the master switch never wired, or (b) no ticks are flowing — cross-check tv_ticks_processed_total. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-6-error-codes.md::AGGREGATOR-HB-01.

panels.title: Gap-fill bars (5m, by outcome)
panels.description: Phase 0 Item 8+9 (PR-D5, 2026-05-17). Per-bar gap-fill outcomes for the 4 IDX_I indices fan-out: succeeded = all 4 SIDs ok; partial = some SIDs failed; failed = zero SIDs succeeded (bar permanently lost until post-market cross-verify catches it). Companion alert: GapFillBarsFailedHigh fires Critical when failed > 0. All series wrapped in increase() per audit-findings Rule 12. Runbook: .claude/rules/project/wave-1-error-codes.md::PHASE2-01 + GAP-FILL-02 in candle_fetcher.rs. Source: crates/core/src/historical/gap_fill_scheduler.rs::execute_bar_for_all_indices.

panels.title: Gap-fill REST latency p99 / DH-904 retries (5m)
panels.description: Phase 0 Item 8+9 (PR-D5, 2026-05-17). Two diagnostic signals for the gap-fill REST path: (1) p99 wall-clock latency of /v2/charts/intraday per attempt (includes both success and error attempts — surfaces Dhan REST tail latency), (2) DH-904 (rate-limit) retry counter split by outcome (retry = backoff fired and we retried; exhausted = retry budget hit and the bar was abandoned). Both increase() per audit-findings Rule 12. Source: crates/core/src/historical/candle_fetcher.rs::fetch_gap_fill_intraday_window. Runbook: .claude/rules/dhan/api-introduction.md rule 8 (DH-904 backoff ladder 10→20→40→80s).
